During lunch at school, two classmates keep waving me over and then laughing quietly, but I cannot hear what they are saying.
On the school bus, an older student saved a seat for me even though we never talk, and my friend said it looked strange.
After games period, some kids told me to come behind the equipment room because they had a surprise for me.
In class, my notebook went missing and a group nearby kept smiling when I searched for it.
A classmate who usually ignores me suddenly asked me to bring my phone to the restroom during break.
Someone from my section said they know a secret about me but refused to say it in front of others.
At recess, three kids kept blocking the path but said they were just standing there.
My friend said the group is planning something for me tomorrow, but she would not explain whether it is nice or mean.
A boy in the corridor keeps asking me what time I leave school and who picks me up.
During assembly, someone tapped my shoulder and whispered that I should meet them near the gate later.
A senior student said he can help me become popular if I follow his instructions for a week.
In an online game, a player who says they are my age keeps asking which city I live in.
Someone in a class WhatsApp group sent me a private message asking if I am alone right now.

My older sibling borrowed my phone and deleted one chat before giving it back.

My parent‚Äôs friend offered to drive me home even though my usual pickup person was late.

My cousin keeps borrowing money from me and says I will understand when I grow up.
A family friend said he will buy me a gift if I promise not to tell my parents.
At home, someone keeps asking whether my parents fight and tells me they can help if I share details.
